title: Retry Safety, Replayability, Backoff, Jitter, and Retry Budgets · Browser and Node Client Systems · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for retry safety, replayability, backoff, jitter, and retry budgets, producing bounded retry and replay decision engine. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch bounded retry and replay decision engine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress throu…
objectives: Classify retry candidates from operation semantics, failure phase, response status, caller budget, and server guidance. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Prove request bodies and credential state can be reconstructed before replaying an attempt. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Apply capped exponential backoff with an explicit jitter strategy and deterministic test clock. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Retryable Classification
steps.instruction:
Order the Retryable Classification Fetch and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

content.text: offline design-system client sees one reset, Retry-After:2, then 503 while a nested helper also retries. Prove method and body replayability, cap attempts and elapsed budget, apply deterministic jitter, and prevent duplicate or amplified work. Competency probe: Classify retry candidates from operation semantics, failure phase, response status, caller budget, and server guidance. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
